fields.description:
RED baseline test suite: inject the full LLM-failure matrix through the pydantic-ai `model_override` seam and pin rebar's CURRENT behavior, so later stories flip it to the new behavior with a regression net.

## Why
Before changing behavior we must pin what rebar does today for every failure class and prove the offline injection harness works. The brainstorm validated on pydantic-ai 1.107.0 that a `FunctionModel` which raises `ModelHTTPError(429)` propagates with `status_code` intact, a canned `finish_reason` is readable at `result.response.finish_reason`, and `httpx.ReadTimeout` propagates unwrapped — so the harness is viable with no live/billable call.

## What
A parametrized RED test file injecting each failure class via a raising or canned pydantic-ai `FunctionModel` through the `PydanticAIRunner(model_override=...)` / `get_runner(override=...)` seams (`src/rebar/llm/runner.py:159,404`), asserting current behavior at two levels: (1) runner classification (everything collapses to `LLMUnavailableError` / `LLMRunnerError` today) and (2) gate end-to-end (plan-review systemic → INDETERMINATE + claim blocked; completion → fail-closed; code-review). Guarded by `pydantic_ai.models.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False`.

## Scope
- Failure matrix: 429-rate, 429-insufficient_quota (body), 529, 500/503, `httpx.ConnectTimeout`, `httpx.ReadTimeout`, 401/403, 400 bad-request, 400 context-length, 413, `content_filter` finish_reason, `ContentFilterError`, length/truncation, `IncompleteToolCall`, unparseable output, validation-retry exhaustion, `UsageLimitExceeded`, `tool_calls_limit`, unknown exception. Each pydantic-ai symbol asserted to exist as imported (a symbol-presence assertion at collection time is itself part of the RED net, so a renamed/absent SDK symbol fails loudly rather than silently skips).
- **`UsageLimitExceeded` fixture uses the DERIVED limit, not the literal 50.** The runner does NOT use pydantic-ai's default `request_limit=50`: it derives `request_limit = max(1, math.ceil(cfg.max_iterations / 2))` at `runner.py:310` (and `tool_calls_limit = max(8, cfg.max_iterations)`). So the looping-model fixture MUST set `cfg.max_iterations` explicitly and assert against the derived value — e.g. `max_iterations=6 → request_limit=3`, asserting the injected looping `FunctionModel` is called `ceil(max_iterations/2)` times before `UsageLimitExceeded` — never a bare `50`. A separate case sets a low `max_iterations` so `tool_calls_limit` (`max(8, max_iterations)`) is the operative cap for a re-calling tool.
- Two-scope scenarios: systemic (whole op) vs per-criterion (one unit among N).
- Named liveness scenarios pin CURRENT (pre-change) behavior only: a hung model request and a hung tool document that TODAY there is no per-request/per-tool timeout (the RED test asserts the current unbounded/hanging behavior, e.g. via a short outer test-level guard), and a looping model reaches the runner's derived `request_limit`. The NEW abort+classify assertions (the `run_sync`-compatible zombie-run defense) are added by story chief-contained-hoopoe's tests, NOT here — this baseline story must stay green on today's code.
- Guard tests: `--force` / `--force-close` / feature-disabled never enters the handling path.
- Coverage boundary: `model_override` injects at the pydantic-ai MODEL layer, which is ABOVE the httpx transport where the retry lives — so transport-layer retry is NOT exercisable here; it is covered by the transport-injection spike in story morbid-uncultured-arcticduck. This suite covers classification + gate behavior + silent-success, not the transport.
- NOT in scope: the NEW behavior (asserted GREEN by later stories) — this story pins CURRENT behavior only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A parametrized test file `tests/unit/test_llm_failure_matrix.py` exercises every failure class via `model_override`, offline, guarded by `ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS=False`; `make test` runs it green on TODAY's code.
- [ ] Each parametrized case pins the current runner-level AND gate-level behavior, and carries an inline `# CURRENT(<file>:<line>): <one-line behavior>` comment naming the exact source location whose behavior it pins (e.g. `# CURRENT(runner.py:365): all provider exceptions collapse to LLMUnavailableError`). "Source citation" = that inline `file:line` comment; it is verified by code inspection (the comment is documentation of the pinned seam, not a runtime assertion) and its presence on every case is checked by a meta-test that greps the test source for a `# CURRENT(` marker per parametrized id.
- [ ] The `UsageLimitExceeded`/`tool_calls_limit` cases set `cfg.max_iterations` explicitly and assert against the DERIVED `request_limit = max(1, ceil(max_iterations/2))` / `tool_calls_limit = max(8, max_iterations)` (runner.py:310-314), never a hardcoded 50.
- [ ] Two-scope (systemic vs per-criterion) and force/disabled guard scenarios are covered.
- [ ] The file is the named baseline that later stories flip to the new behavior; `make lint`/`make typecheck`/`make test` green.
